summary: Set 033 Session 6 of 6 — final session: shipped the cross-tier close-out check-in writer, the three canonical doc updates, the cross-repo CLAUDE.md insertion source, the Set 033 final-session change-log aggregation, dual-registry version bumps + CHANGELOG entries, and the PyPI 0.6.0 release. Marketplace 0.18.0 publish is deferred (see blockers). Writer side: ai_router/session_state.py _flip_state_to_closed now sets state['orchestrator'] = None unconditionally just before the snapshot write — every successful close (mid-set, final, forced) clears the check-out so the next session boundary IS the release point. Idempotent on already-null. Cross-tier: same hot path serves Full close_session and Lightweight hand-write conventions. Tests: three new tests in test_close_session_snapshot_flip.py (final close clear, mid-set close clear, tier-agnostic direct-helper idempotence) + post-close invariant in test_happy_3session.py flipped to assert orchestrator is None. Full ai_router suite: 643 pass + 1 skipped (no regressions). Schema doc (docs/session-state-schema.md): Check-out / check-in (Set 033) section refined — Block-null invariant rewritten for tier symmetry + idempotence; OQ2 alias names work_checked_out / work_checked_in surfaced literally; new Stranded-checkout recovery paragraph naming start_session --force and Release Check-Out as the two release paths. Close-out doc (ai_router/docs/close-out.md): Section 2 gains an Orchestrator check-in paragraph (echoed by --help); Section 3 step 9 mark_session_complete bullet extended to mention the orchestrator clear; Section 4 (failure modes) gains a Stranded check-out failure pattern. Note: spec said 'Section 5 failure modes' but Section 4 is actually the failure-modes section in this doc — adjusted. Workflow doc (docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md): new Orchestrator check-out / check-in (Set 033) subsection under Session-Set Lifecycle and State File codifies within-set sequential + across-set parallel invariants, names force-override as the one explicit deviation, states H4 holder identity composite, calls out tier symmetry; Switching Orchestrators Between Sessions section extended with a pointer at the new subsection. New cross-repo notice (docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md): one-time copy source for dabbler-platform / dabbler-access-harvester / dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb CLAUDE.md files, self-contained snippet between horizontal-rule lines using absolute GitHub URLs for canonical references. New Set 033 change-log (docs/session-sets/033-.../change-log.md): final-session aggregation across S1-S6 per [[project_final_session_changelog_pre_close]] (must pre-exist before close_session). Version bumps: pyproject.toml 0.5.1 -> 0.6.0; tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json 0.17.1 -> 0.18.0; ai_router/CHANGELOG.md 0.6.0 entry + tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/CHANGELOG.md 0.18.0 entry + top-level CLAUDE.md Extension versioning walk extended. Verification: Round A via gemini-pro VERIFIED, no must-fix issues ($0.032; bundle 72,424 chars; eight assertion groups). One nice-to-have applied: writer comment in session_state.py expanded to emphasize the clear is unconditional within _flip_state_to_closed (any future caller inherits the check-in automatically). Round B not needed. Cumulative routed Set 033 spend ~$0.197 of $1.25 NTE. PyPI publish: dabbler-ai-router 0.6.0 LIVE at https://pypi.org/project/dabbler-ai-router/0.6.0/ via .venv/Scripts/python.exe -m build + twine upload; auth required TWINE_USERNAME=__token__ override (operator's $env:PYPI_USERNAME was set to the PyPI account name; PyPI's token-based auth requires the literal '__token__'). In-session incident: a buggy bash idiom (${VAR:+yes}${VAR:-no}) when probing whether the Marketplace PAT env var was set leaked the PAT to stdout; operator rotated the PAT in Azure DevOps; memory entry [[feedback_no_env_var_probing]] saved; the operator is also filing an Anthropic escalation requesting harness-level secret redaction. Marketplace publish (0.18.0) DEFERRED until operator confirms the new PAT is in place. Also remediated mid-session: a stray runtime .dabbler/orchestrator.json marker file at this set's directory (written today by the still-running 0.17.1 extension's codex-config-watcher) was causing the operator's gauge UI to display 'Codex gpt-5.4' instead of the actual Claude Opus check-out. Source on disk was correct; the stale marker was a 0.17.1 reader-side artifact. Deleted the file; the v0.18.0 build neither writes nor reads marker files, so the issue can't recur post-install.
blockers: Marketplace publish of dabbler-ai-orchestration 0.18.0 deferred until operator confirms the new Azure DevOps PAT is in place (rotated after a mid-session leak of the prior PAT to conversation stdout). Once confirmed, run: cd tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration && npx vsce package && npx vsce publish --pat $env:AZURE_VSCODE_MARKETPLACE_TOKEN. Not a Set 033 close-out blocker — the close-out gates don't include 'Marketplace published'.
